title: ranunculus acris var. propinquus
intro: Ranunculus acris var. propinquus, also known as the Meadow Buttercup, is a perennial plant species within the Ranunculus acris genus, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and edible properties. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid content, particularly ranunculin. The health benefits of Ranunculus acris var. propinquus include its ability to aid in the treatment of various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and relieve pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although it's essential to use caution and consult with a medical professional before using it as a treatment.
